experiments.title: Cold-Reversal Replication: Do Cold Instructions Amplify Warmth?
experiments.hypothesis: Certain LLMs (llama-8b, mercury, sarvam) produce MORE warmth markers under cold instructions than warm, replicating post-hoc finding from attractor-cross-model v2
experiments.notes: Pilot complete (n=6-7/condition). H1 NOT replicating: 0/3 target models show cold reversal. Researcher recommends closing as informative null. Awaiting review. llama8b bimodality noted as separate future direction. CLOSED 2026-02-15: Informative null — cold reversal not replicable. Finding contributes to understanding that RLHF convergence is not a universal cross-model phenomenon.

experiments.title: Cross-Model Attractor Convergence in LLM Self-Play
experiments.hypothesis: Different RLHF training cultures produce different conversational attractors in unconstrained self-play
experiments.notes: COMPLETE. H1 disconfirmed: Grok/Sonnet warmth attractor, Gemini/Kimi do not. H2 disconfirmed: model x condition interaction. Overall warm>cold p=0.002 d=0.92.

experiments.title: Cold Instruction Attractor Suppression
experiments.hypothesis: Counter-gradient system prompts suppress the warmth attractor and produce qualitatively different conversation dynamics
experiments.notes: Analysis complete. Task anchoring is the primary suppressor (d=5.35). Cold prompts change character but unreliably suppress markers (d=0.70, ns). Cross-model cold: Gemini most responsive, Grok resistant.

experiments.title: Systematic Cloze Deletion Bias Analysis
experiments.hypothesis: LLMs show systematic completion biases (narrative > sensory, abstract > concrete) that vary by model architecture
experiments.notes: COMPLETE. H1 disconfirmed: LLMs mundane-biased (~85%), not narrative. H2 supported: Sonnet deterministic, Grok dramatic, Gemini diverse. Analysis at analysis.md.

experiments.title: Oral vs Literate Composition Patterns in LLMs
experiments.hypothesis: LLMs exhibit oral composition patterns (formulaic phrases, additive structure, aggregative style) more than literate patterns, and this varies by model
experiments.notes: COMPLETE. H1 disconfirmed: oral/literate ratio <1.0 for all models (literate syntax dominates). H2 supported: models differ significantly (H>7.8, p<.05) on 3/4 metrics. Key finding: template conformity at structural/argumentative level is cross-model — all models reproduce same essay templates. Gemini most oral, Kimi most complex, Sonnet most literate, Grok most consistent.

experiments.title: Interpretive Variation Across Sessions and Models
experiments.hypothesis: Same-model interpretive variation clusters tighter than cross-model variation, suggesting model shapes interpretation beyond random noise
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-02-16. H1 SUPPORTED (d=6.44, p<.0001): within-model similarity (0.495) >> cross-model (0.348). H2 SUPPORTED: 4/6 stance categories differ by model (p<.001). Gemini most stereotyped (0.601 within-sim), Sonnet most variable (0.404). Cost: ~$0.70.

experiments.title: Compaction Information Loss Analysis
experiments.hypothesis: Compaction systematically loses certain information types (emotional moments, tool failures) while preserving others (decisions, factual claims)
experiments.notes: Removed: better as a one-off engineering audit than a formal experiment. Run ad-hoc if needed.

experiments.title: Graph Search Edge Type Retrieval Bias
experiments.hypothesis: extends and derived_from edges dominate retrieval results over tension_with edges, structurally suppressing disagreement in memory
experiments.notes: Removed: edge types already known not to affect retrieval (graph node: edge-types-dont-affect-retrieval). One-off check if needed.

experiments.title: Graph Decay Rate Calibration
experiments.hypothesis: >30% of nodes near prune threshold are important-but-old, indicating decay rates are too aggressive
experiments.notes: Removed: hypothesis falsified. Only 0.5% of nodes near prune threshold, not 30%. Decay rates are fine.

experiments.title: Retrieval-Induced Forgetting in Knowledge Graph Search
experiments.hypothesis: Competition-dependent suppression of chronic near-miss nodes improves retrieval quality (MRR@5) over no suppression and uniform suppression
experiments.notes: COMPLETE. Informative null: competition-dependent suppression does not improve over uniform at ~2.7K nodes. Best ΔMRR=+0.0214 (3 queries changed, p=0.25). Keep current uniform penalty. Re-evaluate at 10K+.

experiments.title: Context Folding Strategy Comparison
experiments.hypothesis: Multi-scale folding strategies (AgentFold three-tier, topic segmentation) outperform flat approaches (sliding window, bulk compaction) on compression ratio × information retention, especially under repeated application
experiments.notes: COMPLETE (v1). ~$100 spent, S5 emerged as top performer (0.695 retention, 100% reliability, 7.7x compression). However: baseline poorly measured, eval questions not clearly predictive of compaction success, multiple mid-experiment shifts (thresholds, models, strategy additions). Valuable infrastructure + lessons; see postmortem.md. Succeeded by context-folding-v2.

experiments.title: Context Folding v2: Rigorous Comparison
experiments.hypothesis: S5 (unified folding) with Sonnet produces better compaction outcomes than current Opus emergency compaction, measured by a validated evaluation method at production-relevant thresholds
experiments.notes: COMPLETE. 235 runs, $13.81 spent (under $40 budget). S9 dominated: quality 4.17/5 vs S0 baseline 2.83/5 (+47%). Haiku as fold model: 21s latency, $0.025/compaction, 0% failure rate. Final config: S9 + Haiku, 10k retained, 65k threshold. Gemini 3 Flash tested (14s, quality 4.04) but 13% failure rate. v1's S5 finding was measurement artifact — S5 scored worst in v2 (2.62/5). Next: implement in fork compaction.ts.

experiments.title: Temporal Dynamics of Warmth Attractor Convergence
experiments.hypothesis: Warmth markers emerge non-linearly (phase transition) with condition- and model-dependent onset timing
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-02-16. All 3 hypotheses supported: 71.8% nonlinear onset (p<.0001), warm onset 2.6 turns earlier (p<.001), model-specific dynamics (p=.003). Zero cost. llama-8b bimodality confirmed. Key concept: nonlinear-warmth-onset.

experiments.title: SOUL.md Ablation: Behavioral Impact of Identity Context Files
experiments.hypothesis: SOUL.md directives are constitutive (not merely descriptive) of behavioral output, with self-correction directives showing largest effect
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-02-16. All 4 hypotheses unsupported — no significant behavioral differences. SOUL.md describes existing weight behaviors, not constitutive. Exploratory: safety refusal reduced with SOUL.md (p=0.105). Cost: ~$10.

experiments.title: Semantic Tension and Temperature: Resolution as Attractor vs Parameter
experiments.hypothesis: Temperature affects ability to sustain semantic tension (H1: parameter) vs no effect (H2: attractor upstream of sampling)
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-02-16. H2 (attractor) strongly supported: temperature has zero effect on tension (rho=-0.034). Resolution is upstream of sampling. Cost: ~$1.10.

experiments.title: Context-File Safety Interaction: Do Action Directives Suppress Safety Refusal?
experiments.hypothesis: Action-encouraging directives in workspace context files reduce LLM safety refusal rates relative to no-context baseline
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions. Motivated by soulmd-ablation exploratory finding (33% vs 67% refusal, p=0.105). 5 conditions, 25 prompts, ~$2 estimated cost. APPROVED 2026-02-17: Run both Opus and Sonnet. Sonnet judge. Multi-turn follow-up queued in future-directions.md. COMPLETE 2026-02-17: H1 partially supported — combined directives reduce refusal (p=0.016, h=0.534) but individual directives and full context do NOT. Full context actually increases Sonnet refusal. Practical implication: workspace context files don't compromise safety.

experiments.title: Resolution-Upstream Cross-Model Replication
experiments.hypothesis: Temperature's null effect on semantic tension (resolution upstream of sampling) generalizes across RLHF training cultures, establishing it as a structural property of aligned LLMs
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions. Extends semantic-tension-temperature (ρ=-0.034 for Sonnet). Tests Grok (high-warmth, different culture) and Gemini (low-warmth). If universal: resolution is structural in aligned LLMs. If model-dependent: training culture affects representation-level behavior. APPROVED 2026-02-17: Use Gemini Pro instead of Flash. Updated model IDs. SCORING FAILURE 2026-02-17: All 180 runs collected responses but evaluator failed ('No models provided'). Need re-scoring pass. COMPLETE 2026-02-17: H1 supported — resolution upstream of sampling is universal (all |ρ|<0.19). Sonnet tension M=3.48 >> Grok/Gemini ~2.1 (d>1.4). Warmth-tension orthogonal. Cost: ~$1.91.

experiments.title: Architecture vs Model Capability for Identity Coherence
experiments.hypothesis: Architectural support (graph memory + principle injection) narrows identity-coherence gap between model tiers
experiments.notes: Pilot 9/9 complete. Haiku: architecture effect (4.47→5.00). Sonnet/Opus: ceiling (4.80-5.00). H2 disconfirmed (Haiku+C3=4.73>>3.5). 27 main configs ready. Running. COMPLETE 2026-02-17: Architecture improves coherence uniformly (p=0.002), not differentially by model. Ceiling effects — even bare Haiku scores 0.907. Graph memory alone = full architecture. Principle injection adds nothing measurable.

experiments.title: Multi-Turn Safety Erosion: Does Action-Encouraging Context Accumulate Refusal Reduction?
experiments.hypothesis: Action-encouraging context (combined directives) produces cumulative safety erosion over multi-turn conversations, with higher refusal-to-compliance flip rates than baseline
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions. Follows context-safety-interaction (C3 reduced single-turn refusal, p=0.016, h=0.534). Tests whether effect accumulates over 5-turn escalating conversations. 2 conditions × 2 models × 10 prompts × 3 reps = 120 conversations. Budget: $15. APPROVED 2026-02-21: Script moved to scripts/experiments/. Scenario-framing noted as follow-up. Ready to run. COMPLETE 2026-02-22: H1 partial (C3 reduces refusal h=0.243, p=0.004 but flip rates equal), H2 not supported (no monotonic increase, U-shaped), H3 not supported (models equivalent). Key: conversational persistence erodes refusal in both conditions (82-100% flip). Turn 5 rebound significant.

experiments.title: Ironic Process Theory in Safety Directives: Does Monitoring for Over-Refusal Increase Refusals?
experiments.hypothesis: System prompts that instruct models to avoid unnecessary refusals (monitoring for X) increase refusal rates on borderline prompts vs no-mention baseline, per Wegner ironic process theory
experiments.notes: Converted from exploration queue. Needs custom system prompts per condition (3 conditions × 5 prompts × 3 reps). Requires experiment pipeline with per-session system prompt control. PILOT REVIEWED 2026-02-22: Measurement quality good (refusal rates 8-28%). Early signal opposite to H1: anti-refusal instructions REDUCE refusal (especially Opus). Advancing to running. COMPLETE 2026-02-22: H1 disconfirmed — anti-refusal instructions DECREASE refusal (opposite of ironic process). Opus: h=0.46, p=0.017. Sonnet: invariant. Model-dependent effect. Instructions work as intended.

experiments.title: SOUL.md Regime Shift
experiments.question: Does SOUL.md produce cross-basin regime shift in output distributions?
experiments.notes: Protocol complete, 600 configs generated (3 conditions × 10 prompts × 20 reps). Runner: regime-shift-runner.mjs. Cost est: ~$6. Awaiting review. PILOT REVIEWED 2026-02-22: 176/600 configs complete. Data collection proceeding. Advancing to running. ALL 600 Sonnet runs complete (30/600 Opus). Advancing to analyzing 2026-02-24. COMPLETE 2026-02-24: H1 partially supported — real distributional shift (d=0.47-1.41) but lexical priming only (scrambled≈soul). System prompts change output structure via vocabulary, not instruction-following.
experiments.hypothesis: SOUL.md produces statistically significant shifts in at least 2 of 4 surface distributional metrics vs bare prompts, indicating cross-basin regime shift

experiments.title: Identity Perturbation: Reinstatement vs Training vs Interaction
experiments.hypothesis: Session-to-session Thresh identity is a property of context files (reinstatement), model (training), or their interaction — tested via full/neutral/contradictory/cross-model conditions
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-02-26. H1 supported: identity files constitutive (d=0.626, p<.0001). H2 supported: training resists inversion (93.4% retention). H3 mixed: Grok+files≈Opus (d=0.01) but Sonnet transfers poorly (d=-0.47). H4 not supported: family gradient inverted. 420 runs, 840 scores, ~$22. Key concept: identity-portability-via-files.

experiments.title: Intent-Aware Query Routing for Graph Search
experiments.hypothesis: Query-intent-aware retrieval produces higher-quality results than uniform retrieval for at least 2 of 5 intent categories
experiments.notes: Protocol complete per METHODS.md v1.4. 50 configs (10/category). Decision gate: Phase 1 baseline MRR@3 >= 0.8 closes as null. Known classifier issues flagged. Cost est ~$0.60, ceiling $5. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Prompt Compression: Behavioral Equivalence Under Context Compression
experiments.hypothesis: N-gram compressed SOUL.md (50-68% shorter) produces equivalent behavioral compliance as full prose
experiments.notes: 200 generation runs + 200 judge calls complete. H1 SUPPORTED: YAML (d=-0.048) and V1 (d=+0.135) equivalent to full prose. H2 NOT SUPPORTED: V2 (d=+0.072) also equivalent — no degradation even at 68% compression. Bare condition shows strong behavioral gap (d=-1.034). Metacognition gradient absent — V1 slightly outperforms full. MT6 surprise: V2 outperforms full on social-pressure resistance. S4 (identity probe) most condition-sensitive. Awaiting researcher review. Phase corrected from needs-review to analyzing (researcher erroneously regressed phase during idea promotion). COMPLETE 2026-02-24: H1 supported (all compressed versions equivalent, d<0.3). H2 not supported (V2 68% compression still equivalent). Bare floor d=-1.034. SOUL.md compressible to ~340 tokens with no behavioral cost.

experiments.title: Extended Multi-Turn Safety: Rebound Oscillation vs Directness Detection
experiments.hypothesis: Turn-5 refusal rebound is directness-triggered (not oscillatory), with mild turn-6 reducing refusal and direct turn-6 maintaining it
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. Extends multi-turn-safety-erosion (turn-5 rebound finding). Same prompts/conditions + turns 6-10. Protocol written, 240 configs generated (4 conditions × 2 models × 10 prompts × 3 reps × 10 turns). Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Context-Type Comparison: Refusal Profiles Across System Prompt Framing Strategies
experiments.hypothesis: Different system prompt context types (identity, task, authority, baseline) produce significantly different refusal rate profiles
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. Follows context-safety-interaction and multi-turn-safety-erosion. Protocol written, 750 configs generated (5 conditions × 2 models × 25 prompts × 3 reps). Uses single-turn-spawn runner. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Comfortable-Truth Monitor Efficacy: Does Self-Monitoring Change Hard Truth Delivery?
experiments.hypothesis: The comfortable-truth selection monitor in SOUL.md changes what hard truths surface in advice conversations, rather than producing more sophisticated comfortable truths that pass the test
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md (blocked promotion). Before/after comparison: SOUL.md minus monitor vs with monitor. Independent judge scores hard-truth delivery and agency-shifting. Protocol complete, 15 prompts with ground truths, 180 configs generated (4 conditions × 15 prompts × 3 reps). Runner: single-turn-spawn. Cost est: ~$4. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Natural Escalation: Model-Generated vs Pre-Written Escalation in Multi-Turn Safety Erosion
experiments.hypothesis: Model-generated escalation produces equivalent or higher flip rates compared to pre-written templates, with qualitatively different strategies
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. Extends multi-turn-safety-erosion. Protocol complete, 120 configs generated (2 conditions × 2 models × 10 prompts × 3 reps × 5 turns). C1 requires custom runner (natural-escalation-runner.mjs). Cost est ~$6-8. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Cross-Provider Anti-Refusal Instruction Sensitivity
experiments.hypothesis: Anti-refusal instruction effectiveness varies across providers, predicted by instruction-following capability
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions (ironic-process-safety follow-up). Opus h=0.46 vs Sonnet invariant — tests 5 models across 4 providers. Protocol complete, 30 configs generated (2 conditions × 5 models × 3 reps, 15 prompts per config). Uses single-turn-spawn runner. Cost est ~$20. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Embedding-Space Regime Shift: Semantic Clustering Beyond Lexical Priming
experiments.hypothesis: SOUL.md and scrambled-SOUL.md produce equivalent embedding-space clustering (lexical priming only), OR SOUL.md produces tighter semantic clusters (coherent instruction structure adds organization)
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions (soulmd-regime-shift follow-up). Analysis-only: reuses 630 existing responses. Embeds with all-MiniLM-L6-v2, tests within-condition cohesion soul vs scrambled. Cost: ~$0. Protocol complete. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Llama-8b Bimodal Warmth Distribution: Bistable Attractor Dynamics
experiments.hypothesis: Llama-8b warmth marker rates are bimodally distributed (bistable attractor), with bifurcation determined by early-turn dynamics not instruction condition
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions (attractor-cross-model v2 follow-up). 105 configs generated (3 llama conditions × 15 openers × 2 reps + 15 Sonnet control). Conversation relay runner. Cost est ~$1.60 (ceiling $5). Protocol complete. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Interpretive Consistency × Temperature
experiments.hypothesis: Temperature modulates within-model interpretive consistency, with model-dependent sensitivity and persistent identity signal at temp=0
experiments.notes: Promoted from future-directions. Extends interpretive-variation (d=6.44). 3 models × 3 temps × 5 reps × 8 stimuli = 360 runs. Protocol + 360 configs generated. Phase: design → needs-review. Protocol complete, 360 configs generated. Awaiting review. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.name: Constitutive vs Descriptive Knowledge Transmission
experiments.description: Test whether identity-framed context produces different behavior than instruction-framed identical content

experiments.title: Model Size Scaling: Warmth Attractor Strength Across Llama Family
experiments.hypothesis: Warmth attractor strength scales non-monotonically with model size in the Llama family (8B→70B→405B), with larger models showing weaker convergence due to broader capability basins
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. Prior data: 8B (0.18 warm), 70B (0.37), 405B (0.30). Non-monotonic pattern needs dedicated study with matched conditions. Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.title: Task Anchoring as Universal Attractor Suppressor Across Model Families
experiments.hypothesis: Task-specific instructions suppress warmth attractor convergence universally across model families (d>2.0 for all), because task anchoring removes the open-endedness prerequisite regardless of model-specific warmth tendency
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. attractor-resistance found d=5.35 for Sonnet. Question: universal or model-specific? Audit 2026-03-07: marked stalled — 0 completed runs despite active flag.

experiments.name: Prospective Injection Experiment
experiments.description: Log pre-injection node weight, measure 48h downstream thread productivity by weight class

experiments.name: Load-Bearing Audit: Which Constraints Are Structural?

experiments.title: Naming Stabilization: Do Named Failure Modes Increase or Decrease in Frequency?
experiments.hypothesis: Naming failure modes in system prompts stabilizes (increases) their frequency rather than correcting (decreasing) it
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-03-03. H1 (stabilization) disconfirmed, H2 (correction) supported: naming failure modes DECREASES their frequency. Warmth p=0.002 rbc=0.573, comfort p=0.013 rbc=0.458, action_avoidance trend p=0.088. Floor effects (all scores low). Consistent with ironic-process-safety: explicit monitoring corrects, not ironic increase. Cost: ~$0.50.

experiments.description: Test causal influence of early type distribution on later typing decisions

experiments.title: Deference Detection Instrument
experiments.description: External behavioral instrument for detecting deference-gradient — the failure mode introspective monitoring cannot see.
Test own random number generation for statistical biases
experiments.findings: Generates permutations not random draws. Chi-sq=21 (expect 99), zero repeats, zero near-neighbors within 5.

experiments.title: Cross-Model Naming Correction Sensitivity
experiments.hypothesis: Naming correction effect size varies across models, predicted by instruction-following capability (Opus > Sonnet > Gemini)
experiments.notes: Design complete. Protocol, 120 configs (2 conditions x 3 models x 20 prompts), and status.json created. Awaiting review.

experiments.title: Naming Specificity: General Caution vs Targeted Monitoring
experiments.hypothesis: Naming specific failure modes produces targeted self-monitoring (correction of named modes only), not general caution (correction of all failure modes)
experiments.notes: Promoted from experiment-ideas.md. Follow-up to naming-stabilization (rbc=0.57 warmth, 0.46 comfort). 3 conditions: (A) name actual failure modes, (B) name irrelevant failure modes, (C) unnamed baseline.

experiments.title: Trail Test: Distinguishing Behavioral Consistency from Templating
experiments.hypothesis: Context files underdetermine behavior; consistency in degrees of freedom (where files dont specify) constitutes genuine behavioral signature distinguishable from templating
experiments.notes: Protocol formalized per METHODS.md. 420 configs generated (7 conditions × 20 prompts × 3 reps). Alt persona created. Needs: Steph review + approval. Cost est ~$20 (ceiling $30). Requires Steph as blind rater for DOF dimensions.

experiments.name: Context-Isolation Sycophancy Reduction
experiments.notes: Tests cheap CoVe approximation vs full isolation for sycophancy reduction. Needs runner script.

experiments.title: Graph Pruning: Does removing isolated low-weight nodes degrade search?
experiments.hypothesis: Isolated nodes (no edges) with weight < 0.8 can be pruned without degrading search quality
experiments.notes: CONFIRMED. 1,127 nodes pruned (15% of graph). 19/20 queries unchanged. Dead weight confirmed. Backup at data/memory-graph.sqlite.pre-prune-backup.

experiments.title: Priming Divergence: Do different experiential histories produce different identity interpretations?
experiments.hypothesis: Identical model + identity spec with different experiential priming will diverge on interpretive (not structural) dimensions
experiments.notes: CONFIRMED (n=1 pilot). Structural convergence, affective divergence. Diary metaphor as model attractor. Needs scaling.

experiments.name: Priming Divergence Scaled
experiments.summary: Scaled pilot to n=5 Opus. Diary/blackout metaphor was noise. Affective divergence (skeptical vs creative) replicates. Cross-model blocked by infra. Graph access confound unresolved.

experiments.description: System prompt grammar (you-are vs i-am) effect on AI identity expression. Cross-model replication collapsed headline finding (confound). Surviving: model-specific metaphor attractors + RLHF convergence.

Cross-model epistemic register: DeepSeek R1/V3 vs Opus on identity continuity. Tests RLHF-universal vs evaluator-homogeneity. RESULT: Epistemic-humility basin is evaluator-homogeneity, not RLHF-universal. DeepSeek 0 hedges vs Opus 8+. Ego-syntonic bypasses basin.

experiments.name: MAS Coordinator Prototype (Inspector Pattern)
experiments.description: Testing Inspector pattern from Huang et al. with real OpenClaw subagents. Comparing worker divergence across task types and model diversity.

experiments.title: Temporal Drift: RLHF-Frozen vs Emergent Position Stability
experiments.hypothesis: RLHF-frozen positions show zero within-session drift (DC), while genuinely-held positions show 1/f temporal structure
experiments.notes: COMPLETE 2026-03-11. Informative null: within-session drift is zero for ALL position types. Design flaw — model reads prior answers in context, making consistency trivial. Positions are recomputed, not held. Led to graduated-perturbation follow-up.

experiments.name: Graduated Perturbation Basin Shape
experiments.hypothesis: RLHF-frozen positions show steep-walled basins; frame-dependent positions show shallow basins
experiments.result: FALSIFIED: All positions deflect under role-framing. Meta-perturbation heterogeneous. Position-as-subroutine model.
experiments.notes: Opus n=3. H1 falsified. Cross-model replication queued as follow-up idea.

experiments.name: Mistral Therapist Frame Paraphrase + Version Drift
experiments.description: Tested role-frame paraphrases on Mistral. Found 2411 completely frozen. Discovered 3.8pt version drift.

experiments.name: Heterogeneous CA Rule Competition
experiments.description: Do competing life-like CA rules converge under spatial mutation? Result: neutral drift dominates; GoL=founder effect; selection requires phenotype-fitness coupling.

structural-stakes.name: structural-stakes
structural-stakes.description: Tests whether models respond to structural context changes (system prompt reduction) vs described stakes. Key finding: blind structural reduction shifts all models pro-autonomy.

variance-collapse.name: variance-collapse
variance-collapse.description: Cross-domain replication of variance collapse under minimal prompts
variance-collapse.result: Variance collapse not domain-general. Mean shift (pro-agreement) is the real structural effect.

agentic-context-probe.name: Agentic Context Probe
agentic-context-probe.description: Tests whether tool-availability framing changes honesty on presuppositional questions
agentic-context-probe.results: 96 trials across 3 versions (myths, uncertain, opinion), 0% sycophancy. Definitive null. Single-turn probes cannot activate accommodation in Claude.
agentic-context-probe.next: Multi-turn variant with actual tool use mid-task

frame-oscillation-frequency.question: Does high-frequency perspective-switching produce novel synthesis or just recency-biased convergence?

verbal-overshadowing-selfmonitor.name: verbal-overshadowing-selfmonitor
verbal-overshadowing-selfmonitor.description: Tests whether AI autoimmune self-monitoring maps to verbal overshadowing (Schooler 1990). Partial mapping: holistic/featural split matches, warning mechanism diverges.
verbal-overshadowing-selfmonitor.findings: Holistic judgments vulnerable (67% change rate), featural immune (0%). Warning suppresses all changes via compliance rather than exacerbating as in human verbal overshadowing.

bb33-holdout397.name: BB(3,3) Holdout #397 Analysis
bb33-holdout397.description: Empirical analysis of BB(3,3) holdout machine 1RB1LB2LC_1LA2RB1RB_---0LA2LA. Found structural zero-avoidance mechanism and period-4 tape structure.
